name: AI Construction Incident Triage & Escalation Management System

parameters.content:
## 🏗️ Construction Site Incident Bridge

### How it works
This workflow acts as a real-time incident hub for construction projects. It accepts incoming issue webhooks from Procore or Fieldwire, normalises the payload into a consistent structure, then uses GPT-4o-mini to classify each incident by type (Safety, RFI, Quality, etc.) and urgency (Critical → Low). Based on urgency, it routes alerts to Slack and email, and logs every incident to Google Sheets. A separate schedule trigger fires at 6 PM daily to pull all open incidents and send a summarised digest to Slack.

### Setup steps
1. **Webhook** — Copy the webhook URL and register it in Procore or Fieldwire as your incident-created event endpoint.
2. **OpenAI** — Add your OpenAI API key credential under the GPT-4o classify node.
3. **Slack** — Connect a Slack OAuth2 account and update the channel IDs in all three Slack nodes to match your project channels.
4. **Gmail** — Connect a Gmail OAuth2 account and replace the PM email placeholder with the real address.
5. **Google Sheets** — Connect Google Sheets OAuth2 and point the two Sheets nodes to your own incident log spreadsheet.
6. **Error Slack channel** — Update `YOUR_SLACK_CHANNEL_ID` in the error handler node.
7. Run a test using the pinned payload (scaffolding fall scenario) to verify the full flow end-to-end.

## 📡 Intake & Normalisation
Accepts webhook events from Procore, Fieldwire, or any compatible form. The Code node maps inconsistent field names (`body`, `subject`, `area`, etc.) into one clean incident object before anything else runs.

## 🤖 AI Classification
GPT-4o-mini reads the incident title, description, location, and raw category, then returns a structured JSON object with `issueType`, `urgency`, `summary`, `recommendedAction`, and `tags`. The follow-up Code node merges this back with the original payload.

## 🔀 Urgency Router
The Switch node splits incidents into four lanes — Critical, High, Medium, and Low (fallback). Critical incidents trigger both Slack @channel and a PM email. High triggers a Slack notice. Medium and Low go straight to the log.

## 🚨 Critical Notifications
For Critical incidents only. Sends a rich Slack block message with `<!channel>` mention and a formatted HTML email to the PM. Both fire in parallel so neither delays the other.

## 📋 Logging & Standard Alerts
Every incident regardless of urgency is appended to the Google Sheets log with status set to `open`. High-urgency incidents also receive a Slack block notice. Medium and Low are logged silently for the daily digest.

## 📅 Daily Digest (6 PM)
A scheduled trigger reads all rows from the incident log and builds a grouped Slack summary — sorted by urgency — showing ID, title, type, location, and assignee for every open item. Runs automatically at 18:00 server time each day.

## 🔐 Credentials & Security
Use OAuth2 for Slack, Gmail, and Google Sheets. OpenAI uses an API key credential. Replace all emails, channel IDs, and spreadsheet IDs with your own before sharing. Never commit real credentials to a shared template.

parameters.message:
=

🚨 Critical Incident Alert

ID{{ $json.id }}
Title{{ $json.title }}
Type{{ $json.issueType }}
Location{{ $json.location }}
Reporter{{ $json.reporter }}
Assignee{{ $json.assignee || 'Unassigned' }}
Summary{{ $json.summary }}
Recommended Action{{ $json.recommendedAction }}
Tags{{ $json.tags.join(', ') }}
Received At{{ $json.receivedAt }}

Sent by Incident Bridge automation — reply to this email to acknowledge.
parameters.subject: =🚨 CRITICAL INCIDENT [{{ $json.id }}]: {{ $json.title }}

parameters.content:
## ⚠️ Error Handler
Catches any failure in the workflow and posts a Slack alert with the error message, failing node name, and execution ID. Wire the error output of any critical node here to prevent silent failures going unnoticed.
